title: The Conscience of a Conservative
description: The Conscience of a Conservative — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Conscience of a Conservative" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.description: "Chapter endnotes and references"

memes.description: "Foreword by Barry Goldwater"

memes.label: "Table of Contents"
memes.description: "Table of Contents"

memes.label: Americans for Democratic Action
memes.description: Left-wing American political organization cited by Barry Goldwater in *The Conscience of a Conservative* as an example of collectivist political activism in the United States.

memes.label: Arthur Larson
memes.description: Arthur Larson (1910–1993) was an Eisenhower administration official and author of *A Republican Looks at His Party* (1956). Goldwater critiques Larson as representative of "Modern Republicanism" — an accommodationist approach that accepted the New Deal framework.

memes.label: Balance of Power
memes.description: The constitutional principle that government power must be distributed among separate branches and levels, creating a system of mutual restraints that prevents any single entity from exercising unchecked authority.

memes.label: Benjamin Franklin
memes.description: Founding Father and Constitutional Convention delegate; Goldwater quoted Franklin's famous warning about the American Republic in The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Big Government
memes.description: The expansion of federal government power beyond its constitutional limits — a central theme in Barry Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative and a foundational concern of modern American conservatism.

memes.label: Bureaucracy
memes.description: The federal bureaucracy as a threat to local control and individual freedom, discussed in Barry Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative in the contexts of welfare administration, education, and federal spending.

memes.label: "I: The Conscience of a Conservative"
memes.description: "The Conservative philosophy and its principles"

memes.label: "II: The Perils of Power"
memes.description: "On the dangers of centralized political power"

memes.label: "III: States' Rights"
memes.description: "The constitutional principle of federalism"

memes.description: "Civil rights and the Conservative approach"

memes.label: "V: Freedom for the Farmer"

memes.label: "VI: Freedom for Labor"
memes.description: "Labor unions and compulsory unionism"

memes.description: "Fiscal conservatism and limited government"

memes.label: "VIII: The Welfare State"
memes.description: "The welfare state and its consequences"

memes.label: "IX: Some Notes on Education"
memes.description: "Federal aid to education and local control"

memes.label: "X: The Soviet Menace"
memes.description: "The Cold War and the Communist threat"

memes.label: Civil Liberties
memes.description: Fundamental individual freedoms that are protected from government infringement, distinguished in Goldwater's framework from civil rights as properly defined — the former are natural rights antecedent to government, while the latter are rights created and enforced by valid laws.

memes.label: Collective Bargaining
memes.description: The process by which labor unions negotiate wages and conditions with employers — Barry Goldwater's views on collective bargaining rights and their limits, from The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: Comprehensive index of all political and philosophical concepts discussed in The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Deficit Spending
memes.description: The practice by which a government spends more money than it receives in revenue, financing the shortfall through borrowing — a fiscal approach that conservatives view as a threat to limited government, sound currency, and individual liberty.

memes.label: Democratic Party
memes.description: One of the two major political parties in the United States, described by Barry Goldwater in *The Conscience of a Conservative* as having abandoned States' Rights for centralized federal power.

memes.label: Document Index
memes.description: Index of all document-type pages in the wiki, covering foundational American legal and political documents referenced in Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Eastern Europe
memes.description: Region of Soviet-dominated states during the Cold War; Goldwater discussed it in the context of American foreign policy and the Soviet Menace in The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Equal Protection
memes.description: The constitutional principle, embodied in the Fourteenth Amendment, that no state shall deny to any person within its jurisdiction the equal protection of the laws — a guarantee of equal treatment under law rather than equal outcomes.

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Index of all historical events discussed in The Conscience of a Conservative and its contextual analysis.

memes.label: Foreign Policy
memes.description: Barry Goldwater's critique of American foreign policy during the Cold War, centered on anti-communism, military strength, and a strategic approach to foreign aid — as articulated in The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Foreign Policy & Cold War Index
memes.description: Index of all wiki pages related to foreign policy, Cold War strategy, and national defense — core themes in Barry Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Fourteenth Amendment
memes.description: Constitutional amendment (1868) guaranteeing equal protection and due process; central to Goldwater's analysis of civil rights and federal power in The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Government Spending
memes.description: Federal government expenditure and its growth — Barry Goldwater's critique of government spending as a threat to individual liberty and fiscal solvency in The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Landrum-Griffin Act
memes.description: Federal labor legislation enacted in 1959; Goldwater analyzed it as a case study in balancing union regulation against government overreach in The Conscience of a Conservative.
aliases: Labor-Management Reporting and Disclosure Act of 1959

memes.label: Harry S. Truman
memes.description: Harry S. Truman (1884–1972) was the 33rd President of the United States (1945–1953). His Fair Deal domestic program and federal budget expansion are cited by Goldwater as examples of the liberal drift in American governance.

memes.label: Henry A. Wallace
memes.description: American politician, 33rd Vice President under FDR (1941–1945), Secretary of Agriculture, and architect of New Deal farm policies that Goldwater criticized as economically destructive.

memes.label: Herbert Hoover
memes.description: Herbert Hoover (1874–1964) was the 31st President of the United States (1929–1933). Goldwater cites the Hoover Commission's recommendations on government efficiency as a model for reducing federal waste.

memes.label: Hoover Commission
memes.description: Federal commission on government efficiency cited by Barry Goldwater in *The Conscience of a Conservative* as an example of cost-saving recommendations that had been largely ignored.
aliases: Commission on Organization of the Executive Branch of the Government

memes.label: Hungarian Revolution of 1956
memes.description: 1956 anti-Soviet uprising in Hungary, crushed by Soviet military intervention; a pivotal Cold War event that Goldwater cited as an example of failed Western resolve.

memes.label: Individual Rights
memes.description: The rights inherent to each person — protected by the Constitution and threatened by government overreach. A foundational concept in Barry Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative and the broader American conservative tradition.

memes.label: Inflation
memes.description: Inflation as a consequence of government fiscal policy, discussed in Barry Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative in connection with deficit spending, union wage demands, and the growth of federal expenditures.

memes.label: John Dewey
memes.description: American philosopher, psychologist, and educational reformer (1859–1952) whose progressive education philosophy Goldwater criticized as a major source of decline in American schooling.

memes.label: Korean War
memes.description: Cold War-era conflict (1950–1953) between North and South Korea, involving United Nations forces led by the United States against communist North Korea and China.

memes.label: Labor Union
memes.description: The economic and political power of organized labor unions as analyzed in Barry Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative, focusing on compulsory unionism, political spending, and threats to individual freedom.

memes.label: Lord Acton
memes.description: British historian and moralist (1834–1902), famous for the maxim "Power tends to corrupt, and absolute power corrupts absolutely," cited by Goldwater in his opening argument against centralized government.

memes.label: Monopoly
memes.description: The concentration of economic power through monopoly as discussed in Barry Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative, including its application to both corporations and labor unions.

memes.label: National Debt
memes.description: The accumulation of federal government borrowing — a central concern of fiscal conservatism and a recurring theme in Barry Goldwater's critique of government expansion in The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: NATO (North Atlantic Treaty Organization)
memes.description: Cold War military alliance discussed by Barry Goldwater in *The Conscience of a Conservative* as a limited but valuable component of America's anti-Communist defense strategy.

memes.label: Nuclear Weapons
memes.description: The role of nuclear weapons in Cold War strategy — Barry Goldwater's views on nuclear superiority, deterrence, and arms control as expressed in The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Organization Index
memes.description: Index of all organizations and institutions referenced in The Conscience of a Conservative, including government bodies, political parties, and international organizations.

memes.label: Person Index
memes.description: Comprehensive index of all individuals referenced in The Conscience of a Conservative, including politicians, philosophers, and historical figures.

memes.label: Regulation
memes.description: Government regulation of economic activity as critiqued in Barry Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative, focusing on its constitutional illegitimacy and impact on individual freedom.

memes.label: Republican Party
memes.description: One of the two major political parties in the United States, critically examined by Barry Goldwater in *The Conscience of a Conservative* for its abandonment of limited-government principles.

memes.label: Republicanism
memes.description: A political philosophy centered on citizenship in a state organized as a republic, where supreme power is held by the people and their elected representatives rather than a monarch, emphasizing civic virtue and the rule of law.

memes.label: Richard Nixon
memes.description: Vice President under Eisenhower and later President; Goldwater quoted Nixon's formulation of moderate Republican conservatism in The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Robert A. Taft
memes.description: Robert A. Taft (1889–1953), known as "Mr. Republican," was a conservative U.S. Senator from Ohio and a leading opponent of the New Deal. His fiscal conservatism and skepticism of federal power expansion are cited in Goldwater's The Conscience of a Conservative.

memes.label: Sam Ervin
memes.description: Sam J. Ervin Jr. (1896–1985) was a Democratic U.S. Senator from North Carolina. Goldwater discusses the Kennedy-Ervin Labor Reform Bill of 1959 as part of his analysis of labor union regulation.

memes.label: Sovereignty
memes.description: The supreme authority of a state to govern itself without external interference, rooted in the principle that legitimate political power derives from the consent of the governed within a defined territory.

memes.label: Supreme Court of the United States
Totalitarianism
memes.description: A system of government in which the state holds total authority over society and seeks to control all aspects of public and private life, based on the principle that the State is competent to do all things and is limited only by the will of those who control it.
